description:
'''Cloud rap''' (also known as '''trillwave''') is a [[Music subgenre|subgenre]] of [[hip-hop]] that emerged from the [[Southern hip-hop]] scene in the late 2000s to early 2010s. The style draws influences from [[trap music|trap]], [[Ambient music|ambient]] and [[New-age music|new age music]] to create a hazy, dreamlike, atmospheric and relaxed production style through the extensive use of reverb and ethereal [[Chopped and screwed|chopped]] samples. It was originally pioneered by artists and producers such as [[Lil B]], [[Clams Casino (musician)|Clams Casino]], [[Friendzone (duo)|Friendzone]], [[Main Attrakionz]], [[Viper (rapper)|Viper]], [[Metro Zu]] and [[SpaceGhostPurrp]].  

In 2010, [[music blog]]ger Walker 'Walkmasterflex' Chambliss who co-founded the blog Space Age Hustle, coined the term "cloud rap" to describe the music of Main Attrakionz member Squadda B. The term was further popularized with their compilation album ''3 Years Ahead: The Cloud Rap Tape.'' In 2011, the genre grew in popularity with the release of [[ASAP Rocky]]'s debut single "[[Peso (song)|Peso]]," followed by "[[Purple Swag]]" and his debut mixtape, ''[[Live. Love. ASAP]]'', which saw production credits from Clams Casino and Friendzone, as well as features from Main Attrakionz and SpaceGhostPurrp.  

In 2013, [[Yung Lean]] released the single "[[Ginseng Strip 2002]]" which went viral online, further popularizing the style. His collective [[Sad Boys]] gained prominence in the cloud rap scene. Other notable artists such as [[Bones (rapper)|Bones]], [[Xavier Wulf]], [[Chris Travis]], [[Night Lovell]] and [[Black Kray]] would become prominent figures in the scene. Cloud rap influenced several [[internet rap]] genres such as [[emo rap]], [[digicore]], [[hexD]], [[Jerk (music genre)|jerk]], [[Rage (music genre)|rage]] and [[sigilkore]].
